title: Ordered CatBoost on Raw High-Cardinality Categorical Structure
summary: This dataset has many drivers and race-driver-compound interactions.
rationale: This dataset has many drivers and race-driver-compound interactions. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered target statistics are specifically designed to reduce prediction shift and leakage from categorical encodings, which is relevant because the current baseline hand-builds many frequency features.
implementation_hint: Run a CatBoostClassifier on mostly raw categorical columns and a compact numeric set, using strict race-aware folds. Compare against the current engineered GBDT as a diversity model, not necessarily a replacement. Test raw combos like Driver x Race, Driver x Compound, and Race x Compound via native categorical handling instead of manual frequency encoding.
expected_effect: May recover signal from high-cardinality interactions with less leakage and provide a meaningfully different model for blending.
risk: If CV is not race-aware and chronologically sensible, CatBoost can still look better locally than it generalizes; keep validation strict.
